zh.homeIntro1: 阿洛伊斯·伊尔迈尔（Alois Irlmaier，1894年6月8日—1959年7月26日），是德国巴伐利亚弗赖拉辛的一名水井探测师（寻水师）和据称拥有灵视能力的先知。他以为当地人寻找水源和失踪物品为生，在二战期间因帮助人们定位失踪亲属和预测空袭而声名鹊起。
zh.homeIntro2: 伊尔迈尔的预言主要集中在一场未来的欧洲大战——他详细描述了俄军从东方入侵、穿越多瑙河、直抵莱茵河的过程，以及随后的三日黑暗和大彗星撞击。这些预言在欧洲天主教预言传统中占有重要地位。
zh.homeIntro3: 他的二战期间预言多有应验记录，1947年曾因「非法占卜」被告上法庭，但法庭审理后裁定其能力真实存在而宣判无罪。本站整理了他最具代表性的预言，并对可验证的条目进行了真实度评估。
zh.coreQuote: 「一切从以色列的事件和中东的纷争开始。一切发生得非常快——我看见三个'九'，但不知道它意味着什么。三日黑暗之后，一切结束了。」
keyPointsList: 1894年 —— 出生于巴伐利亚上施特劳宾
1928年 —— 定居弗赖拉辛，开始水井探测工作
1939-1945年 —— 二战期间为无数家庭寻找失踪亲属
1947年 —— 因占卜被起诉，法庭裁定无罪
1950年代 —— 向多名访客详述未来欧洲大战异象
sourceList: 预言内容主要来源于 Conrad Adlmaier 所著《Blick und Stimmen aus dem Jenseits》（Wikipedia）
部分内容参考 Stephan Berndt 所著《Alois Irlmaier: Ein Mann sagt, was er sieht》（Amazon）
验证评价基于公开新闻报道和历史记录
编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用探水杖符号——伊尔迈尔以寻水师（Wunschelrutengaenger）身份闻名，Y形探水杖是其标志性工具

en.homeIntro1: Alois Irlmaier (June 8, 1894 – July 26, 1959) was a German dowser and alleged clairvoyant from Freilassing, Bavaria. He made his living locating water sources and lost objects for local residents. During World War II, he gained fame for helping people locate missing relatives and reportedly predicting air raids with remarkable accuracy.
en.homeIntro2: Irlmaier's prophecies center on a future European war — he described in vivid detail a Russian invasion from the east, advancing across the Danube to the Rhine, followed by Three Days of Darkness and a great comet strike. These visions hold a significant place in the European Catholic prophetic tradition.
en.homeIntro3: His WWII-era predictions were well-documented. In 1947, he was charged with 'illegal fortune-telling' but was acquitted after the court found his abilities to be genuine. This site compiles his most representative prophecies and evaluates verifiable entries against historical records.
en.coreQuote: "Everything starts with events in Israel and turmoil in the Middle East. It all happens very fast — I see three nines, but I don't know what they mean. After three days of darkness, it is all over."
keyPointsList: 1928 —— Settled in Freilassing, began dowsing work
1939-1945 —— Located missing relatives for countless families during WWII
1947 —— Tried for fortune-telling, acquitted by court
1950s —— Described future European war visions to numerous visitors
sourceList: Prophecies primarily sourced from Conrad Adlmaier's book Blick und Stimmen aus dem Jenseits (Wikipedia)
Additional material from Stephan Berndt's Alois Irlmaier: Ein Mann sagt, was er sieht (Amazon)
Verification based on public news reports and historical records
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus
Site icon: dowsing rod — Irlmaier was known as a Wunschelrutengaenger (dowser), and the Y-shaped divining rod is his signature tool
